findings: WHERE CONVERSIONS HAVE LANDED, IT IS gcrane, NOT PLAIN crane. The bole path (pace T) is fully gcrane: rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh uses gcrane manifest/cp/tag, rbgjs-gcrane-fingerprint.sh uses gcrane manifest, and the single source-of-truth builder constant ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER="gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug" (rbld0_Lode.sh:85) is unambiguously gcrane:debug with an explicit warning comment. Verified: grep for plain '\\bcrane\\b' (excluding gcrane/go-containerregistry) across all .sh/.rs returns ZERO live invocations — the only hit is rbld0_Lode.sh:77 'no crane auth login', a negative reference. So in landed code the answer is yes.

WHY gcrane NOT crane IS LOAD-BEARING (verified via RBSCB:70-71): plain crane authenticates only via authn.DefaultKeychain (docker config), which is EMPTY on a Cloud Build worker — it would FAIL against *.pkg.dev. gcrane uses google.Keychain -> ADC -> GCE metadata server (Mason SA), authing GAR ambiently with no token fetch, no 'crane auth login', no credential-helper image. A mount agent who reaches for plain crane gets a silent/late auth failure.

TERMINOLOGY RISK IN DOWNSTREAM DOCKETS: paces U/V/W/X are described with loose 'crane' / 'crane cp' / 'crane append' wording. A mount agent reading only the docket could grab plain crane. MITIGATION already in place: the ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER constant is the anchor, and the dockets say 'reuse pace T's mechanism' — pace T's landed code IS gcrane, so an agent following that instruction lands correctly. Risk concentrates where a pace introduces a NEW builder row or NEW spec.

RBSPV TERMINOLOGY RISK (pace W) IS REAL AND UNMITIGATED: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/FUTURE/RBSPV-PodmanVmSupplyChain.adoc says plain 'crane' throughout — verified at lines 17, 19, 148 ('crane cp and oras cp both copy a disk leaf to GAR'), 155, 161 ('crane cp-ing selected leaves by digest'), 163 ('Cloud Build carries crane natively'). When W promotes this out of FUTURE/, this 'crane' prose must become 'gcrane' to match the auth reality. The legacy FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh (lines 181-396) installs and uses plain 'crane' inside an ignite VM — that ignite-VM design 'dissolves entirely' per RBSCB; W's greenfield rbldv_ supersedes it and must not resurrect plain crane.

CAPABILITY RISK — 'crane append' IS NOT VERIFIED ANYWHERE (highest-confidence gap): grep for 'crane append' OR 'gcrane append' across the entire repo returns ZERO hits. Pace V's plan converts two FROM-scratch buildx pushes (rbgjl02 vouch = 'FROM scratch'+'COPY vouch.json /', verified at rbgjl02:43-44; rbgjl04 underpin opaque-blob wrap) to 'crane append'. The cerebro experiment (memo) empirically tested only cp/manifest, NOT append. crane append exists in real go-containerregistry and gcrane is a superset, but this is asserted from general knowledge, not repo evidence. V should empirically confirm 'gcrane append' can build+push a FROM-scratch single-layer image on gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug before committing.

BUILDER-ROW REWIRING UNDER-SPECIFIED IN U AND V: moving a step from docker->gcrane requires changing BOTH the builder image AND the entrypoint token (bash->busybox, because gcrane:debug is distroless carrying only /busybox/sh — verified rblds_Spine.sh entrypoint enum extended with busybox at 106-114). Pace U names only rbgjl03-conclave-capture.sh, but conclave's builder row is wired in rbldr_Reliquary.sh:66 (ZRBLD_GOOGLE_DOCKER_BUILDER + bash). Pace V names rbgjl04/rbgjs, but underpin's builder row is in rbldw_Underpin.sh:71-72. These recipe-row co-edits are implied but not enumerated.
docket_fixes_needed: Pace W docket: add explicit instruction to convert RBSPV's plain 'crane' prose to 'gcrane' on promotion out of FUTURE/ (cite lines 148/155/161/163), and to NOT resurrect the legacy ignite-VM plain-crane design from FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh.

Pace V docket: add a pre-implementation verification step — empirically confirm 'gcrane append' can produce and push a FROM-scratch single-layer image on gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug BEFORE relying on it. This is the single highest capability risk in the heat. Flag that this pace may need a chat if append turns out unavailable (fallback question: keep buildx for vouch, or use a different gcrane mechanism).

Pace U docket: name the ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER constant explicitly (not 'crane'), and name the implied builder-row co-edit in rbldr_Reliquary.sh (builder image -> ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER, entrypoint bash -> busybox).
Pace V docket: name the implied builder-row co-edit in rbldw_Underpin.sh:71-72 (builder image + entrypoint flip), not only the step bodies.
All downstream eviction dockets (U/V/X): say 'gcrane' / name ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER rather than bare 'crane', so a mount agent cannot grab plain crane and hit a silent GAR auth failure.

findings: THE EVICTION CHAIN IS STRUCTURALLY SOUND FOR INVOCATIONS: pace T evicted skopeo from the BOLE capture (verified: rbgjl01 is fully gcrane, rbgjs-skopeo-fingerprint.sh deleted, rbgjs-gcrane-fingerprint.sh present). The single remaining LIVE skopeo invocation is the made-side bind mirror (rbgjm01-mirror-image.sh:40 'skopeo copy --all' + rbfd_mirror/zrbfd_mirror_submit), which pace X converts to 'crane cp' (gcrane) and which also retires the inline metadata token fetch. But M also touches zrbfc_resolve_tool_images (rbfca_StepAssembly.sh) — the resolver feeding the made-side build — and no fixture explicitly verifies that a made-side build resolves tool images from the new conclave Lode rather than the retired reliquary. The lifecycle fixtures call divine (RBTDGC_DIVINE_LODES) but no fixture is named to exercise the NEW augur verb or the vouch-envelope decode. If augur is a new operator-facing path, it warrants its own case/assertion. Flag as a potential test gap — N's docket does not name a verify gate.

Housekeeping (mark Windows tracks deferred, resolve DEV-CACHE revert). No code transformation requiring a verify fixture; missing fixture is expected and acceptable.

R teaches the clean-tree convention in onboarding tracks; scope is contingent on where S placed the gate and whether bole election became a separate operator step. handbook-render or onboarding-sequence may cover the prose, but no dedicated gate is named. Low risk given the contingent/doc nature.

X's 'bind-mode build runs green' gate is the rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_bind_plantuml case (onboarding.rs:1114) — but it lives in onboarding-sequence, which runs only in gauntlet/skirmish (NOT service/complete). PLUS hidden test-maintenance: X breaks reliquary-lifecycle (rbtdrc_crucible.rs:3360 asserts rbi_skopeo cohort member) and must update that fixture + drop RBTDRC_RELIQUARY_TAG_SKOPEO const + remove skopeo from ZRBTDRU_GCB_ALLOWED (cupel) — none of this is in X's quoted site list.

D asserts zero skopeo / zero buildx in capture+mirror. The cupel fixture (supply-chain conformance) is the natural enforcement seam, but its allowlist (ZRBTDRU_GCB_ALLOWED) still permits skopeo — so D's assertion needs the cupel allowlist updated (by X or D) to actually enforce. D's 'zero skopeo anywhere' is word-level and will over-fire on legitimate residue (RBSPV podvm survey, CBG teaching examples, historical superseded notes) unless scoped to capture+mirror INVOCATIONS.

findings: The three core capture lifecycle fixtures (lode/reliquary/wsl) EXIST and are service-tier, giving paces T, U, V express, already-built verify gates with no hidden test-authoring scope. This is the strong part of the plan.
The two REAL test gaps that are owned-but-greenfield: W's podvm-lifecycle (correctly owned) and possibly L's platform-fanout assertions (unstated).

The two HIDDEN-SCOPE problems on the verify side both attach to pace X: (a) X breaks the reliquary-lifecycle fixture's rbi_skopeo cohort assertion and must update it + the cupel allowlist + the const — none in X's quoted sites; (b) X's bind-mode verify gate runs only in gauntlet/skirmish, a far more expensive ladder than a service fixture.
Paces N (augur split + rbi_vouch decode) and M (resolver repoint) introduce behavior changes with no explicitly-named verify gate — flag for the operator to decide whether a case is warranted.

open_questions: DONE-but-unwrapped, not genuinely partial: the code eviction is complete and committed (543622648). Only remaining work is running the live-GAR lode-lifecycle fixture green (the fixture already exists). ONE docket-staleness to correct on any reslate: the docket cinched the builder as 'pinned by digest', but T deliberately and operator-blessedly used a FLOATING gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug tag (version-freezing belongs to the reliquary gather, not a scattered bash digest). The 'pinned by digest' cinch should be dropped/corrected.

Fully diagnosed: the false-green mechanism is confirmed (theurge isolates each tabtarget invoke in its own burv_output root via invoke_count at rbtdri_invocation.rs:260-263, so BUK's current/->previous/ promotion never carries the bole touchmark across invokes; zrbfd_elect_base_anchor silently no-ops at rbfd:901). The two fix sites are exactly S's named sites. The only judgment call is HOW theurge should share the previous/ root across invokes without breaking other fixtures' isolation — a design micro-decision the mount agent can make, but worth a one-line heads-up that the fix touches shared test infrastructure.

docker pull/tag/push -> gcrane cp is a clean one-for-one (gcrane cp does registry-to-registry copy, no daemon). The only under-specification is the implied builder-row co-edit in rbldr_Reliquary.sh:66 (builder image + bash->busybox entrypoint), which should be added to the docket but does not require a chat.

CAPABILITY UNVERIFIED: 'crane append' / 'gcrane append' appears ZERO times in the repo and was never empirically tested (cerebro tested only cp/manifest). V must confirm gcrane append can build+push a FROM-scratch single-layer image before committing — if it cannot, the whole pace approach changes (keep buildx for vouch? use a different gcrane mechanism?). This is a chat-worthy fork.

SCOPE BOUNDARY: the buildx snippets V rewrites (rbgjs-buildx-push.sh, rbgjs-buildx-bootstrap.sh) are shared with rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh (hallmark-verify, OUTSIDE the Lode capture family). Does V convert the shared snippet (reaching rbgjv03 too) or fork a Lode-only snippet? D asserts zero buildx in capture+mirror only — rbgjv03 is neither, so a half-converted shared snippet is a real boundary question.
Implied builder-row co-edit in rbldw_Underpin.sh:71-72 (image + entrypoint flip).

Greenfield NEW vertical (verb rbw-lI, rbldv_ body, kind registration, podvm-lifecycle fixture, RBSL podvm subdoc, RBS0 quoin, promote RBSPV out of FUTURE/). This is design work, not mechanical — the shape of the immure operation, the new quoin minting, and the RBSPV terminology reconciliation (plain 'crane' -> 'gcrane') all need judgment. Substantial enough to warrant a design conversation before mount.

Greenfield follow-on to W: extend immure to both quay families + curated multi-platform selection + refresh mode. Depends on W's shape. 'Curated multi-platform selection' and 'refresh mode' semantics are design decisions, not mechanical. Whether L needs its own fixture additions is unstated.

Retire the inscribe reliquary-mirror path and repoint RBRV_RELIQUARY onto a conclave Lode via zrbfc_resolve_tool_images. The repoint is architectural: how the resolver maps reliquary-stamp semantics onto a conclave Lode touchmark, and what 'banish rbi_rq' leaves behind, are judgment calls. Note M and X both touch rbfca_StepAssembly.sh (different lines — M the resolver, X the skopeo line); ordering matters. No named verify gate for the repoint.

Split augur (rbw-la) out of divine and implement rbi_vouch envelope decode. The split point (what stays in divine vs moves to augur) and the envelope-decode format are design decisions. No named verify gate.

README Enshrine/Reliquary -> Lode narrative conversion + RBSHR refresh. Public-facing narrative rewrite is judgment-heavy (what concept vocabulary to surface to external readers). Should reconcile with RBSAE's dead enshrine path — O targets README, but the veiled RBSAE spec describing dead skopeo enshrine creation is the bigger doc problem and is unowned.

findings: Mechanical (mount-ready): T (just needs the live fixture run + a one-line cinch correction), S (fully diagnosed, two named sites), U (clean one-for-one + one implied co-edit), P (housekeeping).

Needs-chat before or during mount: V (unverified crane append + shared-snippet scope boundary), W/L/M/N (greenfield or architectural design), O (public narrative + the unowned RBSAE problem), R (contingent on S), X (hidden cross-pace scope that should be surfaced/decided), D (assertion needs precise scoping).

The heat's mechanical paces are front-loaded (T/S/U) and the design-heavy ones (W/L/M/N) are mid-to-late — a reasonable ordering, but V sits early and carries the single highest capability risk (crane append), so it should get its verification step before the greenfield work begins.

Recommend elevating RBSAE to its own pace (or to O's doc scope), elevating the cupel allowlist + token-fetch file deletion to X (they are eviction consequences), and leaving D as a genuine word-level cross-check with an explicit exemption list.

VERIFY-COST INCONSISTENCY — paces T/U/V/M get cheap service-tier fixtures; pace X's bind-mode gate and pace S's airgap-chain case run only in gauntlet/skirmish (cloud-spending ladders). If the operator expects uniformly cheap gates, X and S are outliers — worth flagging so the wrap criteria are realistic about cost.

SIMPLIFICATION — pace W carries RBSPV's plain-'crane'->'gcrane' reconciliation AND the legacy FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh plain-crane design. Since gcrane-not-crane is load-bearing and already documented (RBSCB:70-71, memo), W could cite the existing ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER anchor rather than re-deriving the auth rationale — reuse the home, don't recreate.
Pace T should not be re-mounted as 'work' — it is done-but-unwrapped. The grooming action for T is a cinch correction (drop 'pinned by digest') plus the live fixture run, not a re-implementation.

recommended_docket_actions.action: Reslate to drop/correct the 'builder pinned by digest' cinch — T deliberately used a floating gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug tag (operator-blessed; version-freezing belongs to the reliquary gather). Note remaining work is only the live-GAR lode-lifecycle fixture run, not code.

Add a pre-implementation verification step to the docket: empirically confirm 'gcrane append' can build+push a FROM-scratch single-layer image on gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug BEFORE relying on it (crane append has ZERO repo evidence). Add the shared-snippet scope-boundary decision (convert shared rbgjs snippet reaching rbgjv03, or fork a Lode-only snippet). Name the implied builder-row co-edit in rbldw_Underpin.sh:71-72 (image + bash->busybox).
Add the implied builder-row co-edit in rbldr_Reliquary.sh:66 (builder image -> ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER, entrypoint bash -> busybox). Name ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER explicitly rather than 'crane'.

Expand X's enumerated scope to cover the hidden test-and-spec maintenance the eviction forces: (1) update reliquary-lifecycle fixture + drop RBTDRC_RELIQUARY_TAG_SKOPEO const (rbtdrc_crucible.rs:2957,3360); (2) remove "skopeo" from ZRBTDRU_GCB_ALLOWED (rbtdru_cupel.rs:152) so the conformance check enforces no-skopeo; (3) delete the now-dead rbgjs-token-fetch.sh file; (4) update LIVE posture claims RBSCB:73/77/218/287 (keep the rejected-helper memo + reference links as history); (5) update the rbhodb_director_bind.sh bind-track handbook narrative (97/181/221/239). Flag the bind-mode verify is gauntlet/skirmish-only (expensive).

Add explicit instruction to convert RBSPV's plain 'crane' prose to 'gcrane' on promotion out of FUTURE/ (cite lines 148/155/161/163) and to not resurrect the legacy ignite-VM plain-crane design from FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh. Cite the existing ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER + RBSCB:70-71 auth rationale rather than re-deriving.

Reslate D's assertion to be precisely scoped: (a) zero skopeo/buildx INVOCATIONS in capture+mirror steps (not every doc mention); (b) EXEMPT FUTURE/RBSPV podvm tool-survey, CBG teaching examples, RBSOB/RBSCB historical superseded notes, RBSIJ generic example; (c) skopeo MANIFEST tool-REFs must be gone (X removed them) while docker MANIFEST tool-REFs are allowed. Remove the cupel-allowlist and token-fetch-file deletion from D's implied load (elevate to X). Add the stale-after-T bole-terminology sweep (RBS0:1456/3565, RBSLC:31, RBSLU:16, acronyms.md:49) with a grep recipe.

Add a pace (or extend O's scope) to retire/rewrite RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc — a LIVE operation spec describing a dead skopeo-driven enshrine-creation path with no rbgje step scripts and no live rbfd_enshrine. This is the largest single hole and exceeds a vocab scrub. Decide whether enshrine-as-creation is formally retired (only wrest/jettison of existing enshrinements survive via rbw-iwe/rbw-iJe) and rewrite or mark the spec accordingly.

Confirm whether the augur split (rbw-la) + rbi_vouch envelope decode warrants its own verify case; no fixture currently exercises the new augur verb. Add a verify gate or note explicit reliance on existing divine coverage.
Clarify whether platform-fanout + refresh-mode need their own fixture additions on top of W's podvm-lifecycle, or extend W's fixture; currently unstated.
Confirm R is reslated after S lands — its scope is explicitly contingent on where S placed the clean-tree gate and whether bole election became a separate operator step.
